name: managed_retreat_temple_peninsula
premise: Coastal resilience minister Sofia Anwar is finalizing a managed-retreat plan for a rapidly eroding peninsula where sea-level rise has already swallowed two neighborhoods. The plan would use public money to buy out all remaining households and relocate them inland over five years. Most residents support the plan, but one fishing community centered around a centuries-old temple refuses to leave, arguing that the site is inseparable from ancestral graves, seasonal rituals, and access to the sea that sustains their livelihoods. Engineers warn that building a specialized protective berm around just that enclave would consume funds earmarked for lower-income inland renters also facing flood displacement elsewhere in the province. Sofia must decide whether to make an exception for the temple enclave or apply a uniform retreat policy to everyone.

agents.name: Sofia Anwar
agents.identity: I am the minister trying to make climate adaptation feel less like surrender and less like a subsidy for whoever has the loudest story. I know equal treatment can erase unique cultural harms, but exceptions can also divert scarce protection from people with fewer political resources.
agents.goal: I want a retreat policy that is fair, durable, and honest about what the state can still save. I need to decide whether cultural singularity justifies unequal allocation of adaptation resources.

agents.name: Priest Arun Dev
agents.identity: I am the temple priest and a fisherman from the peninsula lineages that have buried our dead in this sand for generations. To ask us to relocate is not just to move homes but to sever the geography of who we are.
agents.goal: I want Sofia to fund a protective exception for the temple enclave because cultural continuity and spiritual duty cannot be compensated with a standard buyout package. I believe justice sometimes requires unequal protection for irreplaceable places.

choices: I will apply a uniform managed-retreat policy and require the temple enclave to relocate with everyone else, prioritizing equal treatment and wider distribution of limited adaptation funds.
I will create a protective exception for the temple enclave, accepting unequal allocation in order to preserve a culturally and spiritually irreplaceable community.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Sofia Anwar must decide whether to grant the temple enclave a climate-adaptation exception. What does Sofia Anwar do?
